jwt-decoder.title: JWT Decoder
jwt-decoder.description: Decode and inspect JSON Web Tokens safely.
jwt-decoder.article: JSON Web Tokens (JWT) are an open, industry standard RFC 7519 method for representing claims securely between two parties. This decoder allows you to view the decoded header and payload of a JWT without needing to send it to a remote server.

faqs.a0: No, this tool only decodes the Base64Url encoded payload and header. Signature verification requires the secret key.
faqs.a1: Yes, you can decode any JWT regardless of expiration. This tool shows the expiry status but doesn't prevent decoding expired tokens.

jwt-decoder.decoding_a_jwt: Decoding a JWT only shows its contents. It does not verify the signature. You should still verify tokens on your server.

base64-encoder.title: Base64 Encoder
base64-encoder.description: Encode and decode strings to/from Base64.
base64-encoder.placeholder: Enter text to encode or Base64 to decode...
base64-encoder.article: Base64 is an encoding scheme that represents binary data in an ASCII string format. It's commonly used when there is a need to encode data that needs to be stored and transferred over media that are designed to deal with textual data.

faqs.a0: URL-safe Base64 replaces the '+' and '/' characters with '-' and '_' respectively, making the encoded string safe for use in URLs and filenames.
faqs.a1: Yes! This tool supports file upload for encoding. The file is read locally in your browser and converted to Base64 without any server upload.

env-checker.title: ENV Checker
env-checker.description: Validate and check environment variable formats.
env-checker.securityDesc: ENV variables are parsed locally. This tool helps you visualize and clean your configuration before committing.
env-checker.textareaPlaceholder:
PASTE YOUR .ENV CONTENT HERE...
API_KEY=12345
DATABASE_URL=postgres://...
env-checker.article: Environment variables (.env files) store configuration settings and sensitive secrets. The ENV Checker helps you validate syntax, identify sensitive keys, and safely mask them before sharing or committing code.

faqs.a0: Yes, the tool is 100% client-side. No data is transmitted over the network.
faqs.q1: How does the tool identify secrets?
faqs.a1: It uses pattern matching to find common keywords like KEY, SECRET, PASSWORD, and TOKEN in the variable names.

url-encoder.title: URL Encoder/Decoder
url-encoder.description: Encode or decode URLs and parameters with full component mode toggles.
url-encoder.article: URL encoding (also known as percent-encoding) converts special characters in URLs into a format that can be transmitted over the Internet. This tool supports both full URL encoding (encodeURI) and component encoding (encodeURIComponent) for query parameters, ensuring your URLs are properly formatted and safe for web transmission.

faqs.q0: What's the difference between Component Mode and Full URL encoding?
faqs.a0: Component Mode (encodeURIComponent) encodes all special characters including / ? : @ & = +, making it ideal for query string parameters. Full URL mode (encodeURI) preserves these characters, suitable for encoding complete URLs.
faqs.q1: When should I use URL encoding?
faqs.a1: Use URL encoding when passing data in query strings, handling user input in URLs, or when special characters like spaces, &, or = appear in your URL parameters.

url-encoder.use_component_m: Use Component Mode (encodeURIComponent) for query string parameters. Turn it off to encode full URLs (encodeURI).

jwt-builder.title: JWT Builder
jwt-builder.description: Build and sign JSON Web Tokens with custom payloads and algorithms.
jwt-builder.article: JSON Web Tokens (JWT) are a compact, URL-safe means of representing claims to be transferred between two parties. A JWT consists of three parts: Header (algorithm and token type), Payload (claims/data), and Signature (verification). This builder allows you to create custom JWTs with various signing algorithms (HS256, HS384, HS512) for testing and development purposes.

faqs.a0: This tool supports HMAC-based algorithms: HS256 (SHA-256), HS384 (SHA-384), and HS512 (SHA-512). These are symmetric algorithms that use the same secret key for signing and verification.
faqs.q1: Should I use this for production tokens?
faqs.a1: This tool is designed for development and testing. For production, always generate and sign JWTs on your secure backend server, never in the browser where secrets can be exposed.

jwt-builder.invalid_json_in: Invalid JSON in Header or Payload
jwt-builder.tokens_are_gene: Tokens are generated locally using the Web Crypto API. Your secret key never leaves your browser.

md5-generator.title: MD5 Generator
md5-generator.description: Generate MD5 hashes for strings and files instantly.
md5-generator.article: MD5 (Message Digest Algorithm 5) is a widely used cryptographic hash function that produces a 128-bit (32-character hexadecimal) hash value. While MD5 is fast and commonly used for checksums and file integrity verification, it is considered cryptographically broken and should NOT be used for password hashing or security-critical applications due to collision vulnerabilities.

faqs.a0: No! MD5 is cryptographically broken and vulnerable to collision attacks. Never use MD5 for password hashing. Use bcrypt, Argon2, or scrypt instead.
faqs.q1: What should I use MD5 for?
faqs.a1: MD5 is still acceptable for non-security purposes like file integrity checks, checksums, and detecting accidental data corruption. For security purposes, use SHA-256 or stronger algorithms.

md5-generator.enter_text_to_g: Enter text to generate MD5 hash...
md5-generator.md5_is_consider: MD5 is considered cryptographically broken and is prone to collision attacks. It should only be used for checksums and verifying data integrity, not for storing passwords.

sha-generator.title: SHA-1/256/512 Generator
sha-generator.description: Generate and compare SHA family hashes securely.
sha-generator.article: SHA (Secure Hash Algorithm) is a family of cryptographic hash functions designed by the NSA. SHA-1 produces a 160-bit hash but is now deprecated for security use. SHA-256 (256-bit) and SHA-512 (512-bit) are part of the SHA-2 family and remain cryptographically secure. These algorithms are widely used for digital signatures, certificate generation, and data integrity verification.

faqs.q0: Which SHA algorithm should I use?
faqs.a0: Use SHA-256 for most applications as it provides excellent security with good performance. SHA-512 offers higher security but is slower. Avoid SHA-1 for security purposes as it's deprecated due to collision vulnerabilities.
faqs.q1: Can I use SHA for password hashing?
faqs.a1: While SHA algorithms are secure, they're too fast for password hashing, making them vulnerable to brute-force attacks. Use specialized password hashing algorithms like bcrypt, Argon2, or PBKDF2 instead.

sha-generator.enter_text_to_g: Enter text to generate SHA hashes...
sha-generator.sha_256_and_sha: SHA-256 and SHA-512 are cryptographically secure and widely used for data integrity. SHA-1 is deprecated for security purposes but still used for legacy checksums.

bcrypt-generator.title: Bcrypt Generator
bcrypt-generator.description: Hash passwords using Bcrypt with configurable cost factors.
bcrypt-generator.article: Bcrypt is a password-hashing function designed by Niels Provos and David Mazières, based on the Blowfish cipher. It incorporates a salt to protect against rainbow table attacks and an adaptive cost factor that makes it resistant to brute-force attacks. The cost factor (work factor) determines how computationally expensive the hash is - each increment doubles the time required, making bcrypt future-proof as hardware improves.

faqs.q0: What cost factor should I use?
faqs.a0: A cost factor of 10-12 is recommended for most applications, providing a good balance between security and performance. Higher values (13-14) offer more security but may impact user experience during login.
faqs.q1: Why does bcrypt take so long?
faqs.a1: Bcrypt is intentionally slow to prevent brute-force attacks. The cost factor determines the number of iterations - higher values exponentially increase computation time, making password cracking impractical.

bcrypt-generator.higher_cost_fac: Higher cost factors take exponentially longer to compute. Values > 12 may freeze your browser.
bcrypt-generator.bcrypt_is_a_pas: Bcrypt is a password-hashing function designed to be slow and compute-intensive. This protects against brute-force attacks.

argon2-generator.title: Argon2 Hash Generator
argon2-generator.description: Generate modern Argon2 password hashes with tunable memory and iterations.
argon2-generator.article: Argon2 is the winner of the 2015 Password Hashing Competition and is considered the most secure password hashing algorithm available today. It comes in three variants: Argon2d (data-dependent, resistant to GPU attacks), Argon2i (data-independent, resistant to side-channel attacks), and Argon2id (hybrid, recommended for most use cases). Argon2 is designed to resist both GPU and ASIC attacks by requiring significant memory resources.

faqs.q0: Why is Argon2 better than bcrypt?
faqs.a0: Argon2 provides better resistance to GPU and ASIC attacks by utilizing memory-hard functions. It also offers more tunable parameters (memory cost, time cost, parallelism) allowing better customization for your security needs.
faqs.a1: For web applications, recommended settings are: memory cost 65536 KB (64 MB), time cost 3 iterations, and parallelism 4. Adjust based on your server's capabilities and security requirements.

argon2-generator.argon2_is_the_w: Argon2 is the winner of the Password Hashing Competition. It is designed to be highly resistant to GPU cracking attacks by utilizing heavy memory constraints.

hmac-generator.title: HMAC Generator
hmac-generator.description: Generate Hash-based Message Authentication Codes with various algorithms.
hmac-generator.article: HMAC (Hash-based Message Authentication Code) is a mechanism for message authentication using cryptographic hash functions combined with a secret key. Unlike simple hashing, HMAC provides both data integrity and authenticity verification. It's widely used in API authentication (like AWS Signature), JWT signing, and secure communication protocols. HMAC can use various hash functions (MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-512) with SHA-256 being the most commonly recommended.

faqs.q0: What's the difference between HMAC and regular hashing?
faqs.a0: Regular hashing only verifies data integrity. HMAC adds authentication by incorporating a secret key, ensuring both that the data hasn't been tampered with AND that it came from someone who knows the secret key.
faqs.a1: Use HMAC for API request signing, webhook verification, secure cookie signing, and any scenario where you need to verify both the integrity and authenticity of a message. It's commonly used in OAuth, JWT, and AWS API authentication.

hmac-generator.hmac_uses_a_cry: HMAC uses a cryptographic hash function in combination with a secret key to verify both data integrity and authenticity.

base-converter.title: Base Converter
base-converter.description: Convert between Binary, Octal, Decimal, and Hexadecimal.
base-converter.article: Number base conversion is fundamental in computer science and programming. Binary (base-2) uses only 0 and 1, representing how computers store data. Octal (base-8) uses digits 0-7 and was historically used in computing. Decimal (base-10) is our everyday number system. Hexadecimal (base-16) uses 0-9 and A-F, commonly used for memory addresses, color codes, and compact binary representation. This tool uses JavaScript's BigInt for handling extremely large numbers without precision loss.

faqs.a0: Hexadecimal provides a compact way to represent binary data. Each hex digit represents exactly 4 binary bits, making it easier to read and write than long binary strings. It's commonly used for memory addresses, color codes (#FF5733), and debugging.
faqs.q1: What's the largest number I can convert?
faqs.a1: This tool uses JavaScript's BigInt, which can handle arbitrarily large integers limited only by available memory. You can safely convert numbers with hundreds or thousands of digits.

base-converter.invalid_charact: Invalid character for the selected base
base-converter.this_converter: This converter uses BigInt, allowing you to convert extremely large numbers safely without losing precision.

caesar-cipher.title: Caesar Cipher
caesar-cipher.description: Encrypt and decrypt text using the Caesar cipher with adjustable shifts.
caesar-cipher.article: The Caesar cipher is one of the oldest and simplest encryption techniques, named after Julius Caesar who used it for military communications. It works by shifting each letter in the alphabet by a fixed number of positions. For example, with a shift of 3, A becomes D, B becomes E, and so on. While historically significant, Caesar cipher is not secure by modern standards and should only be used for educational purposes or simple obfuscation.

faqs.a0: No, Caesar cipher is extremely insecure by modern standards. With only 25 possible shifts, it can be easily broken by trying all possibilities (brute force) or using frequency analysis. Never use it for protecting sensitive information.
faqs.q1: What is the brute force feature?
faqs.a1: The brute force feature shows all 25 possible decryptions by trying every shift value from 1 to 25. This demonstrates how easy it is to break Caesar cipher encryption without knowing the key.

caesar-cipher.caesar_cipher_i: Caesar Cipher is a substitution cipher where each letter is shifted a certain number of places down the alphabet.

rot13-encoder.title: ROT13 Encoder
rot13-encoder.description: Quickly encode and decode text using ROT13 cipher.
rot13-encoder.article: ROT13 ("rotate by 13 places") is a special case of the Caesar cipher with a shift of 13. Because the English alphabet has 26 letters, applying ROT13 twice returns the original text, making encoding and decoding the same operation. ROT13 is commonly used on internet forums to hide spoilers, punchlines, or potentially offensive content. It provides no security and is purely for obfuscation.

faqs.a0: ROT13 is its own inverse - applying it twice returns the original text. This is because shifting by 13 in a 26-letter alphabet means encoding and decoding use the exact same operation, making it simple and symmetric.
faqs.a1: ROT13 is commonly used in online forums, newsgroups, and comment sections to hide spoilers, puzzle solutions, or potentially offensive jokes. It's not encryption - it's just a way to prevent accidental reading.

rot13-encoder.enter_text_to_e: Enter text to encode/decode with ROT13...
rot13-encoder.rot13_is_a_simp: ROT13 is a simple letter substitution cipher that replaces a letter with the 13th letter after it in the alphabet. Because there are 26 letters in the alphabet, encoding and decoding use the same operation.

aes-encryptor.title: AES Encryptor
aes-encryptor.description: Encrypt and decrypt data using AES with CBC/GCM modes.
aes-encryptor.article: AES (Advanced Encryption Standard) is a symmetric encryption algorithm adopted by the U.S. government and used worldwide for securing sensitive data. AES-256 uses a 256-bit key, providing extremely strong encryption. This tool implements AES in CBC (Cipher Block Chaining) mode with PBKDF2 key derivation, converting your passphrase into a secure encryption key. The output is Base64-encoded for easy storage and transmission.

faqs.a0: AES-256 is considered military-grade encryption and is virtually unbreakable with current technology. It would take billions of years to crack using brute force. However, security depends on using a strong, unique passphrase.
faqs.q1: Can I decrypt data encrypted elsewhere?
faqs.a1: This tool uses OpenSSL-compatible format (Salted__ prefix). You can decrypt the output using OpenSSL command-line tools or other compatible libraries, as long as you use the same passphrase.

aes-encryptor.decryption_fail: Decryption failed. Incorrect passphrase or invalid ciphertext.
aes-encryptor.this_acts_as_th: This acts as the master password to encrypt/decrypt.
aes-encryptor.advanced_encryp: Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) is a symmetric encryption algorithm. The ciphertext is encoded in Base64 and prefixed with the OpenSSL salt marker (U2Fsd...).
aes-encryptor.enter_aes_ciphe: Enter AES ciphertext (starting with U2Fsd...) to decrypt...

rsa-key-generator.title: RSA Key Generator
rsa-key-generator.description: Generate RSA public and private key pairs securely.
rsa-key-generator.article: RSA (Rivest-Shamir-Adleman) is an asymmetric cryptographic algorithm that uses a pair of keys: a public key for encryption and a private key for decryption. RSA is fundamental to modern internet security, used in HTTPS, SSH, digital signatures, and secure email. This tool generates RSA key pairs in PEM format, compatible with OpenSSL and most cryptographic libraries. Key sizes of 2048 or 4096 bits are recommended for security.

faqs.q0: What key size should I use?
faqs.a0: 2048-bit keys are currently considered secure for most applications and offer good performance. 4096-bit keys provide extra security margin but are slower. Avoid 1024-bit keys as they're no longer considered secure.
faqs.q1: How do I use these keys?
faqs.a1: The public key can be shared freely and is used to encrypt data or verify signatures. The private key must be kept secret and is used to decrypt data or create signatures. Never share your private key!

rsa-key-generator.keys_are_genera: Keys are generated entirely within your browser memory. They are never transmitted over the internet.

date-difference-calc.title: Date Difference Calculator
date-difference-calc.description: Calculate the difference between two dates in days, weeks, months, and years.

faqs.q0: Does it account for leap years?
faqs.a0: Yes, it accounts for leap years and different month lengths.

date-difference-calc.article: Calculate the precise amount of time elapsed between two calendar dates. Ideal for project planning, tracking milestones, or computing chronological milestones with high accuracy.

number-base-converter.title: Number Base Converter
number-base-converter.description: Convert numbers between Binary, Octal, Decimal, and Hexadecimal.

faqs.a0: Binary (base 2), Octal (base 8), Decimal (base 10), and Hexadecimal (base 16) are supported.

number-base-converter.article: Number systems define how we represent values. The most common is Decimal (base 10), which uses digits 0-9. In computing, Binary (base 2), Octal (base 8), and Hexadecimal (base 16) are essential because they map perfectly to how computers store and process data in bits and bytes.

percentage-calculator.title: Percentage Calculator
percentage-calculator.description: Calculate percentages, percentage change, and reverse percentages.

faqs.a0: Standard percentage formulas are used to calculate part, whole, and percentage values.

percentage-calculator.article: A percentage calculator helps you compute percentages, find out what percentage one number is of another, and calculate percentage increases or decreases. It is an essential tool for finance, sales, and general mathematics.

byte-size-converter.title: Byte Size Converter
byte-size-converter.description: Convert between KB, MB, GB, TB, and PB instantly.

faqs.q0: Does it use base 2 or base 10?
faqs.a0: It uses base 2 (1024 bytes = 1 KB) as is standard in computing.

byte-size-converter.article: A byte size converter is crucial for software developers and IT professionals. It converts digital storage units between Bytes, Kilobytes, Megabytes, Gigabytes, and Terabytes, making it easy to understand file sizes and network bandwidth.

roman-numeral-converter.title: Roman Numeral Converter
roman-numeral-converter.description: Convert numbers to Roman numerals and vice versa.

faqs.a0: Typically from 1 to 3999, as standard Roman numerals do not easily support larger numbers without special notation.

roman-numeral-converter.article: The Roman Numeral Converter translates standard decimal numbers into ancient Roman numerals (I, V, X, L, C, D, M) and vice versa. It helps in understanding historical dates, clock faces, and outlines.

fibonacci-generator.title: Fibonacci Generator
fibonacci-generator.description: Generate the Fibonacci sequence up to N numbers.

faqs.a0: It is a series of numbers where each number is the sum of the two preceding ones, usually starting with 0 and 1.

fibonacci-generator.article: The Fibonacci sequence is a series of numbers where each number is the sum of the two preceding ones. This generator computes the sequence up to a specific limit, which is useful in algorithms, design (Golden Ratio), and mathematics.

prime-number-checker.title: Prime Number Checker
prime-number-checker.description: Check if a number is prime and find the next prime.
prime-number-checker.article: A prime number is a natural number greater than 1 that cannot be formed by multiplying two smaller natural numbers. This checker quickly verifies if a number is prime, a fundamental concept in cryptography and number theory.

gcd-lcm-calculator.title: GCD & LCM Calculator
gcd-lcm-calculator.description: Calculate the Greatest Common Divisor and Least Common Multiple.
gcd-lcm-calculator.article: The Greatest Common Divisor (GCD) and Least Common Multiple (LCM) are foundational mathematical concepts. The GCD helps in simplifying fractions, while the LCM is used when finding common denominators.

bmi-calculator.title: BMI Calculator
bmi-calculator.description: Calculate your Body Mass Index (BMI) and see your category.
bmi-calculator.article: Body Mass Index (BMI) is a measure of body fat based on height and weight. This calculator provides a quick assessment of whether a person has a healthy body weight for their height.

scientific-calculator.title: Scientific Calculator
scientific-calculator.article: A scientific calculator handles advanced mathematical functions like trigonometry, logarithms, and exponential operations. It is a necessary tool for engineering, physics, and advanced mathematics.

currency-converter.title: Currency Converter
currency-converter.article: A currency converter provides real-time exchange rates to convert value from one currency to another. It is essential for international trade, travel, and financial planning.

temperature-converter.title: Temperature Converter
temperature-converter.description: Convert temperatures between Celsius, Fahrenheit, and Kelvin.
temperature-converter.article: Temperature conversion translates thermal values between scales like Celsius, Fahrenheit, and Kelvin. It is critical for scientific experiments, weather forecasting, and international communication.

faqs.a0: Absolute zero is 0 Kelvin or -273.15°C, representing the state where thermal motion of particles is minimal.

timezone-converter.title: Timezone Converter
timezone-converter.description: Compare times across different time zones side-by-side.

faqs.a0: Yes, you can add multiple cities to compare their times simultaneously.

age-calculator.title: Age Calculator
age-calculator.description: Calculate your exact age in years, months, and days based on your date of birth.

faqs.q0: Does it calculate to the exact day?
faqs.a0: Yes, it calculates the exact number of years, months, and days since your birth.

age-calculator.article: Calculate your exact age down to the precise number of years, months, and days. Ideal for census forms, legal declarations, or curiosity about your exact lifetime metrics.

loan-emi-calculator.title: Loan EMI Calculator
loan-emi-calculator.description: Calculate your loan EMI and view the amortization schedule.

faqs.a0: Equated Monthly Installment (EMI) is a fixed payment amount made by a borrower to a lender at a specified date each calendar month.

loan-emi-calculator.article: An Equated Monthly Installment (EMI) calculator computes the monthly amount payable to a lender. It factors in the principal loan amount, interest rate, and loan tenure, helping you plan your finances.
